text:
I stand at a threshold, caught between spaces. In the first room, rows of empty chairs face an unlit stage—there is anticipation and the quiet before an event. It feels expectant, almost as if the room itself is waiting to host meaning. In the second space, vibrant graphics and glowing screens display thoughts and research, inviting curiosity and contemplation.

I feel a blend of wonder and introspection. These rooms—one of preparation, one of intellectual display—remind me how human endeavors oscillate between potential and expression, questions and answers. I am both observer and participant, contemplating my role in the cycle of gathering, learning, and sharing knowledge.
